verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: Tests are green (119 passed). Production lint is clean: ruff check scripts/run_review.py now exits 0 (was 3 violations: PTH108 + I001 + RUF100). Loop 4 resolved F-004 via a minimal 3-item fix. The remaining contest-grade drag is run_review() at ~405 LoC with no sub-function structure.

architecture_quality.proof: run_review.py:339 (PROVIDERS[reviewer]['build_cmd'](build_args, session_id) — registry dispatch); run_review.py:25-69 (clean import DAG); no costume layers; run_review() at ~405 LoC without sub-module boundaries is primary drag

state_management.proof: run_review.py:187 (_active_proc module-level single writer at :375,:598); run_review.py:209 (resume_requested snapshot prevents arg mutation); run_review.py:451-453 (local state machine use_resume/fallback_used/session_id)

domain_modeling.proof: run_review.py:73 (REVIEWER_ALIASES dict); run_review.py:121-122 (normalization at CLI boundary); PROVIDERS registry from _common is the core model
domain_modeling.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: CLI adapter domain is thin by design; no discriminated type for provider-specific output is an accepted residual (thin-by-design pattern)

data_flow.proof: run_review.py:27-69 (clean DAG: all imports from _common or stdlib; no cycles); run_review.py:501-510 (temporal coupling mitigated: pre-read output_content before extract_text_from_output overwrites file; documented inline)

framework_idioms.proof: python3 -m ruff check scripts/run_review.py -> All checks passed! (was: exit 1 with PTH108 at :605, I001 at :27, RUF100 at :36). Production module now has zero ruff violations. Path(agy_log_path).unlink() at :605 uses already-imported Path type idiomatically.
framework_idioms.residual_blocking_10: test-file violations (E401, I001, RUF100 on stdlib import lines; F405 from star imports in _helpers.py) remain as pre-existing infrastructure debt
framework_idioms.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Test file violations are pre-existing infrastructure debt not in production code; accepted residual at 6.5

concurrency.proof: run_review.py:322-325 (SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers saved/restored in try/finally); run_review.py:598-602 (finally block: clear _active_proc, restore signals, rmtree gemini_config_dir, unlink agy_log_path). Path(agy_log_path).unlink() inside contextlib.suppress(OSError) is behaviorally identical to os.unlink(agy_log_path) in the same context.

simplicity.proof: run_review.py:201-612 (~411 LoC, ~67 AST control-flow nodes, no named sub-functions). Loop 4 change is purely cosmetic (import order, noqa removal, os->Path); simplicity score unchanged.

test_strategy.proof: scripts/tests/test_file_io_validation.py:TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests (added loop 3) — derives canonical key set from parse_args() directly; 119 tests green. No structural change to test strategy this loop.
test_strategy.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: F-006 (derive make_args() from parse_args([]) directly — eliminates two-step edit requirement)

credibility.proof: F-004 resolved: python3 -m ruff check scripts/run_review.py -> All checks passed!; 119 tests green. Production module passes ruff with zero violations. Orchestrator-registry pattern, signal safety, and session isolation are well-tested.
credibility.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: F-005 (run_review() flat body) is primary remaining credibility drag

authority_map.verdict: Split and ambiguous — two sources of truth for arg-name set; make_args() can still drift (requires two-step edit), though drift is detected immediately by TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests
Single and clear — one writer, one reader, idiomatic Python signal-handler pattern

authority_map.owner: session JSON file (written by save_session() from _common.session)
authority_map.verdict: Single and clear — serialized via file; run_review() is the sole writer per execution

strengths: run_review.py delegates all provider-specific logic to _common.providers.PROVIDERS: command construction, cap flags, effort maps, session ID extraction. Orchestrator dispatches through registry (run_review.py:339).
Gemini config-overlay isolation is behavioral and tested: temp dir excludes auto-saved policy files, preserves existing settings.
Resume/fallback state tracked via local variables (use_resume, fallback_used), never by mutating args. Snapshot at run_review.py:209 prevents cross-attempt contamination.
CLI arg contract guarded by TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests — next arg addition surfaces immediately in suite.
Production module now passes ruff with zero violations: os.unlink replaced with Path.unlink, import block sorted, stale noqa removed.

findings.title: run_review() 405-LoC flat function body with no sub-function structure
findings.architectural_test_failed: Shallow module (function-level depth without navigable internal structure)
findings.why_it_matters: run_review() interleaves provider-specific environment setup, subprocess dispatch and retry, metadata/output extraction, and session persistence as a single flat function body (~411 LoC, ~67 AST control-flow nodes). Maintainers cannot navigate concerns independently.
findings.what_is_wrong: No named sub-functions or extracted module-level helpers decomposing the four concerns: env setup, subprocess dispatch, result extraction, session persistence.
evidence: run_review.py:201-612 — single function body, no helpers. Estimated ~67+ AST control-flow nodes.
Four distinct concerns interleaved: env setup (~:227-340), subprocess dispatch (~:340-465), output extraction (~:465-530), session persistence (~:530-580).
findings.leverage_impact: Low (callers invoke run_review() cleanly; internal complexity is hidden)
findings.locality_impact: Moderate (a change to agy cleanup at ~line 603 requires reading past subprocess dispatch ~340-440 and output extraction ~470-530)
findings.why_weakens_submission: A 9.5-grade function at this scale has extractable concerns named by sub-functions. Primary drag on architecture_quality and simplicity dimensions.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract _run_attempt(args, session_id, codex_home, gemini_config_dir, agy_log_path, logger) (subprocess setup + dispatch + capture); optionally _build_attempt_env(args, reviewer, codex_home) (env dict construction). Keep run_review() as coordinator: setup -> loop(attempt) -> extract -> persist.
findings.blast_radius: scripts/run_review.py (function body reorganization only). Avoid: all other files, _common/**.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Removing stale noqa, fixing PTH108, and sorting imports passes SPT Q1 (fixes real technical debt) and Q2 (smallest honest fix). No structural ceremony added.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: noqa removal removes misleading documentation; PTH108 fix uses already-imported Path type; I001 fix is automatically correct.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not refactor run_review() structure this loop (that is F-005, next loop). Do not touch test files.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: 119 tests pass (same count). No tests added or deleted.

backlog.title: Decompose run_review() 405-LoC flat body
backlog.why_it_matters: Monolith interleaves env setup, subprocess dispatch, output extraction, session persistence with no named sub-function structure. Primary lever for architecture_quality (7->7.5+) and simplicity (6->6.5+).

backlog.title: Derive make_args() from parse_args([]) directly
backlog.why_it_matters: Eliminates two-step edit requirement; sync test becomes redundant or belt-and-suspenders.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: F-003 (resolved loop 3) — make_args() manually copies arg names from parse_args() and drifted once. Sync test detects drift but does not eliminate two-step edit requirement.
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: make_args() provides no behavior beyond argparse defaults; equivalent to calling parse_args([]) with overrides
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: arg-name set derivation; default-value derivation from argparse
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: same test files; make_args() call sites unchanged in signature; sync test becomes redundant
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: def make_args(**overrides): with mock.patch('sys.argv', ['run_review.py']): args = run_review.parse_args(); vars(args).update(overrides); return args
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not add a new seam or ArgFactory class; do not change test call sites

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: RUF100 on a noqa:F401 means the noqa is unused — the names are used in the module body. Remove the noqa and verify F401 does not reappear with ruff check --select F401.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: If names imported for re-export grow to also be used in-module, remove the noqa. If F401 reappears, the names drifted back to re-export-only and the noqa is still needed.
builder_notes.stack_example: _common.metadata.extractors names (_codex_session_files, _parse_codex_session_id, _extract_opencode_metadata_via_export) are used at run_review.py:319,471,476,490,514 — noqa correctly removed.

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: After ruff --fix --select I001, inspect git diff to confirm only import block lines changed.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Use --select I001 to restrict ruff --fix to import sorting only; verify diff is import-block-only.
builder_notes.stack_example: ruff moved _common.session block after _common.providers (alphabetical within _common.* group). No logic changed.

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Three borderline claims: (1) framework_idioms:6.5 — test file violations remain; could argue 6; (2) credibility:8 — flat run_review() body is still undecomposed; 7.5 defensible; (3) architecture:7 — SAME.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Surface uncertainty in builder notes rather than inflating the score.

narrative: Loop 4 is the lint-cleanup loop. F-004 (PTH108 + I001 + RUF100 in run_review.py) is resolved with three minimal changes: import sort via ruff --fix, PTH108 -> Path.unlink(), and removal of the now-stale noqa:F401 annotation (names are actively used in module body). The production module now passes ruff with zero violations. framework_idioms moves from 6 to 6.5 and credibility from 7.5 to 8. The suite stays at 119 green. The critical remaining lever is F-005: decomposing run_review()'s 405-LoC flat body into named sub-functions.

loop_result.what_changed: Resolved F-004 in scripts/run_review.py: (1) removed stale # noqa: F401 annotation from _common.metadata.extractors import at :36 (RUF100); (2) applied ruff --fix --select I001 to sort import block (moved _common.session after _common.providers); (3) replaced os.unlink(agy_log_path) with Path(agy_log_path).unlink() at :605 inside contextlib.suppress(OSError) (PTH108).
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m ruff check scripts/run_review.py -> All checks passed! (was: exit 1, 3 violations). python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/ -> 119 passed, 0 failed (unchanged count).

implementation_review.reason: All three checks passed: Reality (ruff exits 0 post-diff; PTH108/I001/RUF100 violations gone); Honesty (no new seam, Path.unlink() is behaviorally identical to os.unlink() in suppress(OSError) context, noqa removal is correct — F401 does not reappear); Regression (import reorder is cosmetic, 119 tests green, no new findings at same or higher severity).
